name: Lemon-Lattice White Chocolate Cake
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Plus 1 tbsp unsalted butter, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
FROM: Derek Maddox (The Tally System - 912-328-6183)
LEMON CURD
Combine lemon juice and cornstarch in a heavy medium saucepan. Stir until cornstarch dissolves. Whisk in remaining ingredients. Cook over medium heat until the mixture is thick and smooth and just begins to boil, stirring constantly for about 7 minutes. Transfer to a medium bowl. Cover with plastic wrap directly on top of the curd to prevent skin from forming. Refrigerate until chilled, about 6 hours. (Can be prepared up to 2 days in advance.)
FROSTING
Combine 1/2 cup cream and chocolate in a heavy small saucepan. Stir over low heat until chocolate melts and mixture is smooth. Transfer to a large bowl. Whisk in the remaining 1-3/4 cups cream and vanilla. Refrigerate until well chilled, about 6 hours. (Can be prepared up to 1 day in advance.)
CAKE
Preheat oven to 350°F. Position rack in the center.
Butter three 9-inch diameter cake pans with 1-1/2 inch high sides. Line bottoms with waxed paper. Butter the paper and dust the pans with flour, tapping out excess.
Sift together 2 cups of all-purpose flour, baking powder, and salt into a medium bowl. Repeat sifting.
Stir chocolate and 1/2 cup cream in a heavy medium saucepan over low heat until chocolate melts and mixture is smooth. Mix in remaining 1/2 cup cream, milk, and vanilla.
Using an electric mixer, beat butter and 1 cup sugar in a large bowl until fluffy. Beat in yolks. Stir dry ingredients into the butter mixture alternately with the chocolate mixture, beginning and ending with dry ingredients.
Using an electric mixer fitted with clean, dry beaters, beat egg whites in a medium bowl to soft peaks. Gradually beat in remaining 1 cup sugar. Continue beating until stiff but not dry. Fold whites into cake batter in two additions. Divide batter among prepared cake pans. Bake until a tester inserted in the center comes out clean and cakes begin to pull away from the sides of the pans, about 24 minutes. Cool cakes in pans on racks for 10 minutes. Turn cakes out onto racks, peel off waxed paper, and cool completely. (Can be prepared up to 4 hours in advance. Cover and let stand at room temperature.)
Beat frosting in another large bowl until stiff peaks form. Place one cake layer on a platter. Spread 2/3 cup lemon curd evenly atop the cake layer. Spread 3/4 cup frosting atop the curd. Top with second cake layer. Spread cake with 2/3 cup curd, then with 3/4 cup frosting. Top with third cake layer. Frost top and sides of entire cake evenly with remaining 3 cups frosting.
Cover remaining lemon curd in a pastry bag fitted with a #2 star tip. Pipe a circle of curd around the top of the cake, about 1/2 inch from the edge. Pipe evenly spaced diagonal lines inside the circle, creating a lattice pattern. (Reserve remaining curd for another use.)
Spoon remaining frosting into a clean pastry bag fitted with a #2 star tip. Pipe a ruffled border around the top and bottom edges of the cake.
(Can be prepared up to 6 hours in advance. Refrigerate.) Let stand 30 minutes at room temperature before serving.
